preamble: The shift from a single vowel (monophthong) to a two-part vowel sound (diphthong) is known as diphthongization or vowel breaking. It often occurs as a result of vowel lengthening or as part of a chain shift (like the English Great Vowel Shift).

examples.note: Diphthongization of Vulgar Latin [ɔ], related process

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Wells, J. C. (1982). Accents of English.
